headline: angrath.md attributes the Rosewater Amass-on-Angrath quote to Blogatog Tumblr Q&A; the actual primary source is the magic.wizards.com 'War Games' article (Making Magic, 2019-04-22), not Blogatog (2 loci in node + 1 in upstream card trivia)
summary: cards/_characters/angrath.md line 39 reads: 'Per Rosewater's Blogatog clarification, the Amass mechanic appears on Angrath not because he's allied with Bolas but because his magic \"sways others temporarily to his side\" — he's pulling Eternals from the Dreadhorde to fight for him mid-battle, not serving Bolas's chain of command.' Line 62 (Canonical sources): 'Mark Rosewater Blogatog (Tumblr) — Rosewater Q&A explanation that Amass on Angrath represents his sway-others-temporarily magic, NOT alliance with Bolas. The clarification is the single most BBL-relevant lore detail for the WAR-227 card.' WebSearch + WebFetch verification: the quoted phrasing 'sways others temporarily to his side' IS authentic Rosewater, but its primary source is 'War Games' by Mark Rosewater on magic.wizards.com (2019-04-22), NOT a Blogatog Tumblr post. The 'War Games' article verbatim: 'his power is about swaying others temporarily to his side (meaning he could use the army without necessarily working with Bolas).' The article also frames Angrath as the ideal black-red walker choice among the available options (Angrath, Ob Nixilis, Tibalt) for thematic fit with Amass. Origin of the misattribution: the upstream card trivia at cards/magic-the-gathering/war-of-the-spark/227-angrath-captain-of-chaos.md line 95 cites '[MTG Wiki: Angrath, Captain of Chaos, citing Rosewater Blogatog (Tumblr)]' — the card-trivia author trusted MTG Wiki's secondary-source attribution to Blogatog without verifying that the primary source is the magic.wizards.com article. The node body then imported the wrong attribution from the card trivia (same upstream→layer-node-confab pathology as CBG-002, CBG-005, CBG-009, CBG-012).

evidence.verbatim_quote_match: 'sways others temporarily to his side' appears verbatim in 'War Games' (magic.wizards.com, 2019-04-22). The quote itself is real and accurately rendered in the node.

wrong_source_citation.current: Mark Rosewater Blogatog (Tumblr) (https://markrosewater.tumblr.com/) — Rosewater Q&A explanation that Amass on Angrath represents his sway-others-temporarily magic
wrong_source_citation.correct: Mark Rosewater — 'War Games' (Making Magic, magic.wizards.com, 2019-04-22) — Rosewater's primary design-article explanation that Amass on Angrath represents his sway-others-temporarily magic, NOT alliance with Bolas. The article identifies Angrath as the ideal black-red walker for the Amass mechanic from the three available BR walker options (Angrath, Ob Nixilis, Tibalt).

evidence.upstream_trivia_carrier: cards/magic-the-gathering/war-of-the-spark/227-angrath-captain-of-chaos.md line 95 — same Blogatog misattribution at the card-trivia layer, citing MTG Wiki as the secondary source. Fix at the card layer: replace '[MTG Wiki: Angrath, Captain of Chaos, citing Rosewater Blogatog (Tumblr)]' with '[magic.wizards.com: \"War Games\", Mark Rosewater, 2019-04-22]'.

fix.approach: 3 surgical edits across 2 files: 2 in angrath.md (lines 39 + 62), 1 in 227-angrath-captain-of-chaos.md (line 95). Preserve the quote text verbatim — only the source citation changes.

edits.old: Per Rosewater's Blogatog clarification, the Amass mechanic appears on Angrath not because he's allied with Bolas but because his magic \"sways others temporarily to his side\"
edits.new: Per Rosewater's \"War Games\" (Making Magic, 2019-04-22), the Amass mechanic appears on Angrath not because he's allied with Bolas but because his magic \"sways others temporarily to his side\"

edits.old: **Mark Rosewater Blogatog (Tumblr)** (`https://markrosewater.tumblr.com/`) — Rosewater Q&A explanation that Amass on Angrath represents his sway-others-temporarily magic, NOT alliance with Bolas. The clarification is the single most BBL-relevant lore detail for the WAR-227 card: Angrath isn't loyal to Bolas, he's using Bolas's Dreadhorde the way he used the Devil's Chains crew — as labor he can compel.
edits.new: **Mark Rosewater — \"War Games\"** (Making Magic, `https://magic.wizards.com/en/news/making-magic/war-games-2019-04-22`, 2019-04-22) — Rosewater's primary design-article explanation that Amass on Angrath represents his sway-others-temporarily magic, NOT alliance with Bolas. Rosewater identifies Angrath as the ideal choice among the three available black-red walkers (Angrath, Ob Nixilis, Tibalt) for thematic fit with the Amass mechanic. The clarification is the single most BBL-relevant lore detail for the WAR-227 card: Angrath isn't loyal to Bolas, he's using Bolas's Dreadhorde the way he used the Devil's Chains crew — as labor he can compel.

edits.old: **Design (why Amass on Angrath)** — Rosewater clarified on Blogatog that Amass appears on Angrath not because he's allied with Bolas, but because Angrath's magic \"sways others temporarily to his side\" — he's pulling Eternals to fight for him mid-battle, not serving the Dreadhorde `[MTG Wiki: Angrath, Captain of Chaos, citing Rosewater Blogatog (Tumblr)]`.
edits.new: **Design (why Amass on Angrath)** — Rosewater explained in his \"War Games\" design article that Amass appears on Angrath not because he's allied with Bolas, but because Angrath's magic \"sways others temporarily to his side\" — he's pulling Eternals to fight for him mid-battle, not serving the Dreadhorde. Rosewater identifies Angrath as the ideal BR walker choice for Amass among the three available options (Angrath, Ob Nixilis, Tibalt) `[magic.wizards.com: \"War Games\", Mark Rosewater, 2019-04-22]`.

neighbor_spotcheck: Cross-checked the 10 other corpus files that mention Blogatog (cards\magic-the-gathering\war-of-the-spark\167-mowu-loyal-companion.md, cards\magic-the-gathering\modern-horizons-3\31-indebted-spirit.md + 150-eldrazi-repurposer.md, cards\magic-the-gathering\magic-2015-m15\239-will-forged-golem.md, cards\magic-the-gathering\duskmourn-house-of-horror\189-manifest-dread.md, cards\magic-the-gathering\aetherdrift\146-skycrash.md + 112-adrenaline-jockey.md, cards\_symbols\manifest-dread.md). None carry the same Angrath-Amass quote misattribution; their Blogatog citations are for different quote/topic pairs that need independent verification but are not THIS finding's territory.
isolation_check: Pattern is isolated to the Angrath-Amass quote-source thread (3 loci across 2 files). Not corpus-wide. The MTG Wiki Angrath page's secondary-attribution to Blogatog is the seed; any future trivia or layer-node commission citing 'Rosewater on Blogatog' should WebFetch the primary article before merging.
pattern_alert: Same anti-pattern family as CBG-009, CBG-012, CBG-016 — designer-source-claim drift from card trivia into layer-node bodies. The expanded pathology here is secondary-source trust: when MTG Wiki attributes a Rosewater quote to Blogatog, future commissions should WebFetch the primary article (which Wiki may not name) before merging Blogatog as the citation. The same quote often appears in BOTH a Making Magic article AND a Blogatog post; the article is the canonical citable source.
